mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST above the 6-month moving average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 12-month moving average signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO below the 12-month moving average are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 0.3% signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE above 0.3% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above the 12-month moving average signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 above the 12-month moving average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 2-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a weakening supply chain and a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both INDPRO below the 12-month moving average and HOUST above the 6-month moving average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and a 2-month decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a tightening monetary cycle and a weakening demand for goods and services, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and GDPC1 below the 12-month moving average are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 0.3% and a 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening labor market and a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE above 0.3% and INDPRO below the 12-month moving average are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
